summary: Full backend + frontend regression of FlowSpace AI room-organization additive build. 22/22 pytest API tests pass (auth gating, auth/me Bearer+cookie, projects 402 for free over-limit, project 400 invalid room/style, billing config + checkout returning live Stripe URL, admin login + all 7 tabs + affiliate CRUD). UI Playwright run verified: landing preserved with all CTA testids, /app/{,new,billing} redirect to /login when logged out, seeded cookie session loads Dashboard with credits/plan, /app/new shows all selectors, /app/billing renders 3 plans with current=pro and billing-choose-premium triggers redirect to checkout.stripe.com (LIVE cs_live_* session — NOT completed), FREE over-limit user sees new-no-credits banner and disabled generate button, trilingual EN/ES/PT switching works on landing, admin login (garage2025) loads dashboard with all tabs and affiliate add (TEST_AdminProduct) appears in list. NO project was actually created via the UI to avoid Replicate cost — E2E pipeline already validated by test_e2e_pipeline.py.
critical_code_review_comments: Stripe webhook secret (STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET) is empty in backend/.env — backend currently parses webhook payload WITHOUT signature verification (falls back to json.loads), which is insecure on a LIVE Stripe account. MUST set STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET after deploy.

/api/projects credit consumption is up-front + refund-on-failure (good), but there is a small race window where two concurrent POSTs from the same user could both pass the 'used >= limit' check before either increments — consider an atomic findOneAndUpdate with conditional $inc to enforce limit transactionally.

auth.py /api/auth/session inserts a new user_sessions doc on every login without deleting prior sessions for the same user — over time will accumulate stale sessions (low priority; sessions auto-expire via expires_at check).

Billing /api/billing/checkout uses payload.origin_url from the client untrusted — an attacker could craft a session whose success_url points elsewhere. Consider deriving origin from request.headers['origin'] or pinning to REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL.
projects.py _process imports asyncio via __import__('asyncio') inside the function — should be a top-level `import asyncio` for readability (cosmetic).
Landing CTAs and app pages are well-instrumented with data-testids, dashboard-title, lang-switcher, billing-choose-*, admin tabs — testability is excellent.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (7/7 UI flows incl. admin + billing redirect)

test_credentials: Admin password garage2025 (verified). User auth via seeded session_token cookie (TEST_* prefix) per /app/auth_testing.md.
seed_data_creation: Created and cleaned up: 2 TEST_ users (1 pro w/ credits, 1 free at limit) + 2 user_sessions + 1 TEST_AdminProduct affiliate row. One live Stripe checkout session (cs_live_b1JMf58M...) was created during the billing-choose-premium click test; NO card details entered and NO payment completed. payment_transactions has the corresponding 'initiated' row.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test_flowspace_api.py is the canonical regression suite (22 tests, ~4s). test_e2e_pipeline.py is the cost-incurring (Replicate+GPT+PDF) verification — DO NOT run it casually. To run UI auth-gated tests, seed a users+user_sessions row (see /app/auth_testing.md) and add the session_token cookie with domain space-transform-59.preview.emergentagent.com, httpOnly, secure, sameSite=None. The PRO test user's billing-choose-pro button is intentionally MISSING (replaced by billing-current-pro) since the user is already on Pro — this is correct behavior, not a bug. Stripe keys are LIVE — always intercept or stop after seeing checkout.stripe.com URL.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. All requested flows behave correctly. Only forward-looking risks noted in critical_code_review_comments (STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET missing, untrusted origin_url, credit-limit race window).
